hook.text: Two hospitals want to train an AI on their combined patient data. But neither can share the raw records. Now what?

buildup.text: Privacy-preserving computation lets parties compute on data they can't see. The data stays encrypted during processing.

discovery.text: Techniques include homomorphic encryption, secure enclaves, and federated learning — all emerging in cloud services.

twist.text: These techniques are still slow. Homomorphic encryption can be millions of times slower than normal computing.

climax.text: As regulation tightens, privacy-preserving computation will become essential. The technology is catching up to the need.

punchline.text: Compute on data you can't see. Privacy and insight coexist.

quiz.question: What is the main limitation of privacy-preserving computation today?
options: The techniques are extremely slow and computationally expensive
It only works with numerical data, not text or images
Cloud providers have banned all privacy-preserving techniques
